2604.27637.title: Optimization before Evaluation: Evaluation with Unoptimised Prompts Can be Misleading
2604.27637.paper_summary: Current Large Language Model (LLM) evaluation frameworks utilize the same static prompt template across all models under evaluation. This differs from the common industry practice of using prompt optimization (PO) techniques to optimize the prompt for each model to maximize application performance. In this paper, we investigate the effect of PO towards LLM evaluations. Our results on public academic and internal industry benchmarks show that PO greatly affects the final ranking of models. This highlights the importance of practitioners performing PO per model when conducting evaluations to choose the best model for a given task.
2604.27637.paper_summary_zh: 目前的大型語言模型（LLM）評估框架在所有被評估模型中使用相同的靜態提示模板。這與業界普遍採用的提示優化（PO）技術以優化每個模型的提示以最大化應用性能的做法不同。在本文中，我們研究了PO對LLM評估的影響。我們在公共學術和內部行業基準上的結果顯示，PO對模型的最終排名有很大影響。這突顯了從業者在進行評估時，針對每個模型進行PO的重要性，以選擇適合特定任務的最佳模型。
2604.27637.author: Nicholas Sadjoli et.al.
2604.27637.authors: Nicholas Sadjoli, Tim Siefken, Atin Ghosh, Yifan Mai, Daniel Dahlmeier
